items.q: Why do informal settlements develop in rapidly growing LIC cities?
items.correct: Rural-urban migration outpaces formal housing supply; migrants build their own homes on marginal land without secure land tenure — driven by housing shortage, not by personal choice.
items.misconception: People live in slums because they prefer them or are too lazy to find better housing.
items.random_wrong: Informal settlements are built by the government for new migrants.
items.why: Self-construction is a response to formal-sector failure. Many residents are employed and pay informal rent. Students moralise; the geographical analysis is about supply, tenure and land economics.

items.q: What weather does an anticyclone (high pressure system) bring?
items.correct: Descending air warms and dries, suppressing cloud formation — so anticyclones bring clear, settled weather: hot in summer, cold and frosty in winter.
items.misconception: High pressure brings stormy weather because the air pushes down hard.
items.random_wrong: Anticyclones bring heavy rainfall because they trap moisture.
items.why: Students conflate 'high pressure' with 'strong weather'. In fact, sinking air evaporates clouds — depressions (low pressure with rising air) bring storms, not anticyclones.

items.q: What is the enhanced greenhouse effect?
items.correct: The strengthening of the natural greenhouse effect by human activities (mainly burning fossil fuels) which add CO2 and other gases to the atmosphere.
items.misconception: The greenhouse effect is entirely man-made and harmful.
items.random_wrong: It is the hole in the ozone layer letting in more sun.
items.why: The natural greenhouse effect is essential for life — without it Earth would be -18 degrees C. The problem is enhancement. Students also confuse it with the (separate) ozone hole.

items.correct: A type of slum in Brazil
items.why: It's A type of slum in Brazil. “A market in India” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which is part of the INFORMAL economy?
items.correct: Street vendor with no business permit
items.why: It's Street vendor with no business permit. “Office worker in a bank” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately what PERCENTAGE of Rio's population lives in favelas?
items.why: The real figure is 20. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
